ac:rich-text-body.p: The SKA Subarray Templates Library is a collection of pre-configured subarray templates for common observation types. Templates encapsulate optimal array configurations, frequency bands, correlator settings, and data rates, allowing observers to quickly set up feasible observations without detailed array engineering knowledge.

p: A subarray is an independent partition of the SKA telescope that can observe simultaneously while other subarrays observe different targets or conduct different science. Subarrays provide:

ul.li: Parallel Observations: Multiple science teams use the array concurrently
Flexible Resource Allocation: Distribute dishes/stations across different science goals
Dedicated Pipelines: Each subarray has independent data processing chains
Optimised Configurations: Tailor array geometry, frequency, and bandwidth to specific science

p: Rather than designing subarrays from scratch each time, the Templates Library provides ready-made configurations tested and optimised by the Observatory.
The Library contains templates organised by observation type and array stage. Each template includes:

ul.li: Template Name: Descriptive label (e.g., "SKA-Mid Continuum Survey – AA2")
Science Use Case: Primary science enabled by the template
Array Stage: Applicable Array Assembly stage (AA1, AA2, AA*, AA4)
Dish/Station Count: Number of elements in the subarray
Correlator Configuration: Channel count, spectral resolution, polarisation
Data Rate: Typical data rate in Gbps
Expected Sensitivity: Approximate 1-sigma noise at standard integration time

tr.td: All available dishes/stations for maximum sensitivity and resolution. Recommended for targeted deep surveys and transient searches.
SKA-Mid Full Array Survey, SKA-Low Full Array HI Survey
Array partitioned into 2–4 independent subarrays for simultaneous multi-target observations. Each subarray is smaller but operates in parallel.
SKA-Mid Dual-Array Spectroscopy, SKA-Low Split-4 Transient Search
Dedicated subarray piggybacking on primary observations, sharing data streams. Enables science without dedicated observing time.
SKA-Mid Timing Array (commensal), SKA-Low Transient Monitor (commensal)
Dense central cluster of antennas/dishes for high surface brightness sensitivity. Ideal for extended source studies.
SKA-Low Extended Source Survey (core), SKA-Mid Giant Radio Galaxy Mapping
Sparse outer baselines for high angular resolution. Suitable for compact sources and interferometric imaging.
SKA-Mid High-Resolution AGN Imaging, SKA-Low Radio Quasar Structure

h2: Example Templates by Science Use Case

ul.li: Best For: Large-area continuum surveys, extragalactic source detection, AGN searches
Sensitivity: ~20 mJy per 1-hour integration (per 10 kHz channel)
Best For: HI cosmology, galaxy surveys, neutral hydrogen mapping
Dishes: 32 (dedicated subarray, commensal to primary science)
Correlator: 1k channels, 500 kHz resolution (coarse for low computational load)
Data Rate: 5 Gbps (low overhead for commensal operation)
Best For: Pulsar timing, gravitational wave detection, pulsar studies
Configuration: 197 dishes split into 2 independent subarrays
Subarray A: 100 dishes, Frequency: 6.5–10 GHz, 1 target
Subarray B: 97 dishes, Frequency: 3.5–5 GHz, simultaneous different target
Sensitivity: ~5 µJy per subarray, per 10-hour integration
Best For: Molecular line studies, redshift surveys, simultaneous multi-object spectroscopy
Dishes: All 197 in full extended configuration
Best For: Compact source structure, AGN jets, radio quasar imaging, VLBI-class resolution

p: Different templates are available at each Array Assembly stage as the observatory grows:

tr.td: Core continuum surveys, deep pointed observations, pulsar timing (commensal), HI pilot studies
Full-array continuum, extended HI surveys, dual-subarray spectroscopy, survey-depth imaging
Optimised continuum (2.7 GHz BW), multi-subarray operations, extended surveys, commensal science expanded
All templates; maximum sensitivity, resolution, and parallel observing capabilities

h2: How to Use Templates in Observation Planning
p: Visit the Templates Library in the SKA Proposal Handling Tool (PHT) or this portal. Filter by:

ul.li: Array Assembly stage (your preferred deployment phase)

p: Each template page includes a detailed description, expected sensitivity, beam properties, and data rates. Use the SKA Sensitivity Calculator to estimate sensitivity for your specific integration time.
In PHT, select "Create Observation from Template" and choose your desired template. The template pre-populates:
Use the SKA Set Up Validator to check your observation configuration for feasibility. Once validated, submit your proposal.

ac:rich-text-body.p: Templates are starting points. You can modify correlator settings, bandwidth, frequency, or subarray composition within limits. The Set Up Validator will flag any modifications that violate array capabilities for your chosen Array Assembly stage.

p: For each template, detailed technical documentation is available including:

ul.li: Sensitivity Tables: Predicted noise levels at various integration times and elevations
Beam Characteristics: Beam size, shape, and sidelobe levels across the field
Correlator Specifications: Exact channel count, tuning resolution, Stokes product configuration
Data Products: Raw data format, metadata content, calibration data included
Example Observations: Sample proposals using this template (with PI permission)

ac:rich-text-body.p: The Templates Library is updated regularly as hardware is commissioned, correlator capabilities expand, and new science use cases are identified. Consult the Observatory documentation for the latest available templates and array configurations.

If you cannot find a suitable template for your science goals, contact the SKA Observatory observing support team via the PHT help system. The team can advise on template selection, custom modifications, or proposal feasibility.
